prestazioni: Engine swap to Toyota-sourced 1NZ-FE (1.5L) with a custom-built turbo kit featuring a Garrett GT2860RS turbocharger, HKS 264° camshafts, and a Tomei 4-2-1 stainless steel exhaust manifold for optimized spool and top-end power, paired with a Haltech Elite 2500 ECU for precise fuel and ignition mapping (targeting 220 HP at 7,500 RPM with 98 RON fuel).

Full drivetrain reinforcement including a Cusco 1.5-way limited-slip differential (LSD) for the front-wheel-drive layout, reinforced driveshafts with Toyota RAV4 CV joints, and a KAAZ 2-way clutch-type LSD for the rear axle (if converted to AWD via a custom Subaru Impreza rear subframe and drivetrain integration).

Suspension overhaul with Ohlins Road & Track DFV coilovers (custom valved for the Trezia’s 2,550mm wheelbase), Whiteline adjustable front sway bar (24mm hollow), and SuperPro poly bushings (front control arms, subframe, and rear trailing arms) to eliminate compliance and improve turn-in response.
estetica: Liberal Motors ‘Stance’ widebody conversion kit (custom-fabricated for the Trezia’s unique body lines), featuring extended front fenders (30mm per side), rear quarter panels (40mm per side), and a carbon fiber rear diffuser with integrated dual-exit exhaust cutouts for the HKS Hi-Power Spec-L system.

Custom aero package including a Varis Japan front lip spoiler (modified to fit the Trezia’s bumper contours), a Seibon carbon fiber hood with a functional NACA duct for the intercooler, and a custom rear wing based on the Rocket Bunny ‘Pandem’ design (30mm taller than stock, with adjustable angle mounts).

Wheel setup with Work Emotion CR Kiwami in 17x8.5 +35 front and 17x9.5 +30 rear, wrapped in Yokohama Advan Neova AD09 tires (215/40/17 front, 245/35/17 rear), paired with DBA 4000 Series rotors and Project Mu B-Spec brake pads for aggressive track-capable stopping power.
